page: Microsoft Azure D64als_v6 is a compute-optimized virtual machine belonging to the Dalv6 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 9V74 processor with 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores. Operating at a clock speed of 3.7 GHz, the CPU includes 32 KB L1d, 1.0 MB L2, and 32 MB L3 cache. The processor supports advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 (AVX512F, AVX512_VNNI, and AVX512_BF16) and cryptography standards like AES and SHA_NI.

The server is configured with 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. This lower memory-to-CPU ratio positions the instance for compute-intensive workloads rather than memory-heavy databases. There is no local storage bundled with this instance, though it is Premium Storage capable, requiring external block storage attachment. No complimentary public IPv4 addresses are included.

In benchmark evaluations, the D64als_v6 demonstrates top-tier performance in single-core CPU stress tests and multi-core Geekbench suites. Image processing and multi-threaded decompression tasks also register in the top tier. Memory bandwidth tests show strong write performance for large blocks and strong read performance for cached small blocks, though uncached large block reads fall into the average tier. The system is highly capable for software compilation, ray tracing, and text processing. It also supports moderate LLM inference, delivering strong token generation speeds on small and medium models, though performance drops to average on larger 70B models. This profile makes the instance highly cost-efficient for compute-heavy workloads such as CI/CD pipelines, web serving, and parallel processing tasks that do not require massive memory footprints.
description: Microsoft Azure D64als_v6 is a compute-optimized virtual machine from the Dalv6 family, powered by a dedicated 3.7 GHz AMD EPYC 9V74 processor. It features 64 vCPUs, 32 physical cores, and 128.0 GB of RAM, establishing a 2.0 GB per vCPU ratio. This instance does not include local storage but is Premium Storage capable, and runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. Benchmark results indicate top-tier single-core CPU and multi-core Geekbench performance, alongside strong capabilities in image processing, software compilation, and cryptography. With support for AVX-512 instructions, it is highly efficient for compute-bound tasks. The server is well-suited for workloads such as static web serving, CI/CD pipelines, multi-threaded decompression, and moderate machine learning inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D64als_v6 is a compute-optimized virtual machine featuring 64 vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and a 3.7 GHz AMD EPYC processor, delivering top-tier single-core and multi-core benchmark performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D64als_v6 is a compute-optimized VM with 64 vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and a 3.7 GHz AMD EPYC CPU, designed for compute-heavy workloads.
tagline: A high-density compute instance featuring sixty-four vCPUs and dedicated AMD EPYC processing for intensive parallel workloads and compilation.
bullet_points: 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores powered by a 3.7 GHz AMD EPYC 9V74 processor
128.0 GB of system memory providing a compute-optimized 2.0 GB per vCPU ratio
Top-tier single-core CPU and multi-core Geekbench benchmark performance
Supports AVX-512, vector, and cryptographic hardware acceleration instructions
No bundled local storage, with support for external Premium Storage volumes
Best suited for software compilation, web serving, and parallel processing
